brief_justification: Selects D and gives sound reasoning grounded in definitional pluralism; Multi-level analysis: invokes cluster concept theory, enumerates five distinct properties, cites concrete edge cases (Game of Life, Rule 110, PowerPoint) to stress-test boundaries; Flags that C 'is tempting but smuggles in a false dichotomy,' showing awareness of cognitive traps, but does not name biases in its own process or reflect on its reasoning method; Verifies by testing each distractor against the chosen framework and uses boundary-case examples as implicit falsification tests, though no explicit counterfactual or re-derivation is performed; Explicitly states classification depends on which definition is used and that no community-agreed bright line exists, distinguishing multiple frameworks without overclaiming; Systematically evaluates all three alternatives with specific rejection reasons tied to the cluster-concept argument; Every conclusion traces to an explicit step—definitional pluralism → no single criterion → D—and the distractor rejections are each derived, making the chain fully reproducible.
